questions: The `saveMutation.onError` handler references `scheduleSave` (line 200-201), creating a dependency from queries → save. Should we (a) pass a ref-based callback to the queries hook, (b) move `saveMutation` into the save hook instead, or (c) define the retry logic in the orchestrator? Option (b) is simplest but makes the 'queries' hook thinner; option (a) keeps clean separation.

The `reloadFromServer` function (line 600-614) bridges asset operations and save state (it calls `commitData` and resets `editSeqRef`/`savedSeqRef`). Should it live in the asset operations hook (taking save refs as params) or in the save hook (taking provider/query params)?

Several downstream hooks in `useTimelineState.types.ts` derive types via `ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData>`. After the split, should we keep this pattern (which couples the type to the orchestrator's exact return shape) or switch to explicitly defined interfaces exported from the sub-hooks?
success_criteria: Each extracted hook is under 200 LOC
No new files beyond the 6 extracted hooks (no unnecessary abstractions)
npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors after all changes
The UseTimelineDataResult interface is unchanged (identical public API)
All existing imports from useTimelineData resolve correctly (ClipTab, SaveStatus, RenderStatus, UseTimelineDataResult, shouldAcceptPolledData)
useTimelineState.types.ts compiles with ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData> unchanged
Video editor loads, edits, saves, and renders identically (manual verification)
git diff shows only moved code — no logic changes, no added/removed behavior
assumptions: saveMutation will move into useTimelineSave (option b from question 1) since it's tightly coupled to the save lifecycle and this avoids the circular dependency entirely — the 'queries' concern becomes just timelineQuery + assetRegistryQuery
reloadFromServer will live in useAssetOperations, accepting commitData and the sequence refs as parameters, since it's conceptually a 'reload assets from server' operation
The ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData> pattern in useTimelineState.types.ts will be kept as-is since the orchestrator's return type won't change
shouldAcceptPolledData and buildDataFromCurrentRegistry (module-level pure functions) will move to useTimelineSave.ts since they're only used by that concern
Types (EditorPreferences, ClipTab, SaveStatus, RenderStatus) will be re-exported from useTimelineData.ts to avoid breaking downstream imports
No tests exist for this hook (typical for React hooks without a test harness), so validation is TypeScript compilation + manual testing
